# Fixing a Slow Next.js Monorepo Build: An Enterprise CI/CD Case Study

**Keyword:** slow nextjs monorepo build
**Date:** 2026-04-07
**Words:** 841
**QA:** 88/100

---

 Fixing a Slow Next.js Monorepo Build: An  engineering case study  CI/CD Case Study  1. Incident Report: The 45-Minute Deployment Bottleneck  At FouriqTech, we recently led an architecture overhaul for a global fintech client managing 14 distinct applications in a single repository. Across 150 engineers pushing 80+ PRs daily, a severely slow nextjs monorepo build was paralyzing their deployment pipeline. The production CI/CD process averaged an execution time of 45m 12s, with p95 build times spiking to 52m during peak load. This latency translated to over $450,000 in annualized developer idle time and critically delayed zero-day CVE patches in their  saas - b2b saas frontend -architecture-multi-tenant-scale">B2B  saas  frontend architecture .  2. Wrong Assumptions  The internal DevOps team assumed the bottleneck was purely compute-bound. In an attempt at immediate ci/cd pipeline optimization, they migrated their GitHub Actions runners from standard 4-core instances to dedicated 32-core self-hosted EC2 instances. They also implemented a standard github actions cache targeting the  node_modules  directory. These infrastructure adjustments reduced build times by a mere 2 minutes. CPU utilization metrics revealed the 32-core runners were operating at just 15% capacity. Throwing bare metal at the problem was an expensive misdirection.  3. Debugging Process: Tracing the Thread  To isolate the real bottleneck, we initiated a comprehensive  React performance audit  on the build phase itself. We instrumented the CI pipeline with  ndb  and injected a webpack bundle analyzer into the Next.js build lifecycle. By monitoring memory consumption via  process.memoryUsage() , we traced the execution hang directly to the production build script ( next build ). At exactly the 12-minute mark, the Node.js process emitted a fatal diagnostic:  FATAL ERROR: Ineffective mark-compacts near heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory .  4. Root Cause  The slow nextjs monorepo build was not caused by a lack of CPU power, but by aggressive V8 Garbage Collection (GC) thrashing and disabled compiler optimizations.    V8 Memory Limitations:  The Node.js runtime was hitting its default 4GB heap limit. When the heap saturated, the V8 engine paused compilation to run continuous, ineffective GC cycles, consuming 90% of the active build time.   Compiler Fallback:  A legacy plugin in  next.config.js  was silently disabling swc minification, forcing Next.js to fall back to the significantly slower Terser compiler.   Chunking Inefficiency:  Webpack was generating 800MB of duplicate shared UI component chunks across the 14 apps because the shared workspace dependencies were not correctly isolated.   5. Implementation Fix  We executed a multi-layered fix to bypass the heap limits, force Rust-based compilation, and implement distributed build caching.   // 1. next.config.js optimizations
module.exports = {
  swcMinify: true,
  output: 'standalone', // Enables next.js stand
